sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Looking at the image, there's now a 'G' frame above the player's white head. The player's white head has shrunk. Let me check the segmentation to see what changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("Player position:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("\nFrames:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'G', 'b', 'S']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
So pressing SPACE above the center-below frame created a 'G' frame at row 24-27, col 32-35. The white head shrank from [[24, 32], [24, 35], [28, 35], [28, 32]] to [[28, 32], [28, 35]].
This is the same pattern as before: pressing SPACE creates a 'G' frame, and the white head shrinks.
Now I have two 'G' frames: one at row 24-27, col 32-35 (above the center-below frame) and one at row 20-23, col 16-19 (above the left frame, from earlier).
Wait, let me check if the 'G' frame from the left frame is still there. Looking at the segmentation, I don't see a 'G' frame at col 16-19. The left frame is still 'c' at row 28-31, col 16-19.
So the 'G' frames are temporary and disappear when I move away or press SPACE again.
Let me think about what the goal could be. The right frame was collected (it's no longer visible). Maybe the goal is to collect all three frames (left, center, and center-below).
But I don't understand how to collect the frames. The right frame was collected automatically when I moved to a specific position, or maybe I pressed SPACE at the right time.
Let me try pressing SPACE again to see if it collects the 'G' frame.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try pressing SPACE again to collect the 'G' frame
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
